title: Idea: URDF Embodiment-Prior World Model
description: Idea-stage world-model direction: import URDF as an embodiment prior and compile URDF/sim replay into projected control videos, masks, and joint-token sidecars for controllable sim-to-real robot video generation.
summary: URDF should act as a structured articulated-object / embodiment prior. OSCAR suggests the most practical interface: use URDF + joint trajectory + camera metadata to produce FK-projected 2D skeleton/control videos and sidecars, then condition a video world model rather than asking the model to understand raw URDF text alone.
details: Core idea: build a video world model that can import a URDF, treat the robot as an articulated object, and generate action-conditioned rollouts from joint-angle or joint-action controls.

Embodiment prior: URDF should expose link geometry, joint graph, joint type, limits, kinematic chains, FK geometry, action/state dimensionality, and joint semantics. This is the prior that tells the model what kind of body can move and how it can move.

Difference from standard AC-WM: many action-conditioned world models assume a fixed robot body, fixed action semantics, or one dataset-specific action dimension. The URDF route separates embodiment prior from object/scene dynamics, so different robots can inject different-dimensional actions into a shared video-generation backbone.

Interface sketch: scene/context video + URDF-conditioned articulated subject + per-timestep action vector -> controllable video rollout. The conditioning interface likely needs variable-length joint tokens, masks, joint-name/type embeddings, action adapters, and FK/limit-aware positional priors rather than one fixed action head.

Evaluation sketch: compare fixed-embodiment AC-WM against URDF-conditioned generation on seen URDFs, held-out URDFs, and variable-action-dimension masking. Metrics should include joint-limit consistency, FK-projected silhouette/pose error, contact plausibility, and IDM recoverability.

Placement decision 2026-06-11: keep this as Idea/Survey material, not part of the current Tri-View execution scope. Tri-View should remain scoped to UMI Ego World, Robot Video Layers, and Layered Data Utility.

OSCAR update 2026-06-27: this idea now has a concrete nearby reference. OSCAR fine-tunes a video world foundation model for physical AI by using robot-control conditions derived from simulator traces; the key lesson is to turn URDF and joint replay into aligned visual controls instead of treating URDF as a prompt token.

Updated interface sketch: URDF + simulator replay / per-timestep joint state + camera intrinsics/extrinsics -> FK-projected 2D skeleton or link-line control video, optional segmentation/depth/contact sidecars, variable-length joint tokens, and masks -> controlled robot video rollout. Raw URDF remains metadata for provenance and validation; the learned model sees compact spatial controls plus action tokens.

X/status observation 2026-06-27: a sim replay can be converted into 2D skeleton control videos for OSCAR-style generation. That is the right bridge for this card: URDF import becomes a control-video compiler and verifier, not necessarily a new generative backbone.

First probe to promote the idea: choose one small manipulator URDF, run a short simulator replay, export skeleton/control video plus joint/action manifest, feed it to an OSCAR-style or Cosmos/WAN control branch, and evaluate whether generated video obeys link pose, joint limits, contact timing, and scene appearance better than raw sim RGB or text-only conditioning.

references.title: URDF robot description: links, joints, limits, and virtual joints
references.notes: Support reference for treating URDF as structured robot morphology: physical joints, link geometry, joint limits, and optional virtual/root joints.

references.title: Gamma-World: Generative Multi-Agent World Modeling Beyond Two Players
references.notes: Nearby world-model baseline: synchronized action-conditioned interactive generation. The URDF idea differs by focusing on cross-embodiment morphology/action injection rather than only multi-agent scaling.

references.title: One Hand to Rule Them All: Canonical Representations for Unified Dexterous Manipulation
references.notes: Relevant boundary reference for morphology priors and cross-embodiment action representations, especially canonical URDF-style hand unification.

references.title: OSCAR: Scaling 'Sim-to-Real' Video Generation for Physical AI
references.notes: Closest build-upon reference for this idea: URDF/simulator-derived robot control conditions are converted into visual control signals for sim-to-real robot video generation.

references.title: arXiv: Scaling 'Sim-to-Real' Video Generation for Physical AI
references.notes: Use for method and evaluation details around action-conditioned robot video generation and sim-to-real visual transfer.

references.title: X post: sim replay to 2D skeleton control videos for OSCAR video generation
references.notes: Design clue from the shared status: simulator replay can be compiled into 2D skeleton control videos, matching the proposed URDF-to-control-sidecar interface.

risks_decisions: Feasibility risk: any-embodiment generation needs data or simulation coverage across multiple URDFs; without that, it becomes a fixed-embodiment AC-WM with extra metadata.
Architecture risk: variable action dimensions require tokenized/adapted action interfaces, not a single fixed MLP action head.
Promotion condition: move out of Idea only if there is a concrete dataset, model interface, and evaluation that cannot be handled by the current Tri-View three-subproject scope.

Decision 2026-06-27: reframe URDF import as a control-sidecar compiler: URDF/FK/sim replay should produce skeleton videos, masks, depth/contact sidecars, and joint-token manifests for a video generator. Do not rely on raw URDF text conditioning as the primary interface.

OSCAR overlap risk: if OSCAR already covers URDF/sim-replay-to-real-video for fixed robots, this card must differentiate on cross-embodiment generalization, variable joint/action dimensions, and URDF-level validation across held-out morphologies.
Data risk: the idea needs paired sim replay/control sidecars and real videos across multiple embodiments; otherwise it stays an OSCAR-style fixed-embodiment sim-to-real finetune.
asset_alt: URDF embodiment prior world model idea
asset_caption: Updated idea sketch: URDF morphology plus sim replay compiles into FK-projected 2D skeleton/control videos and sidecars for controlled sim-to-real video generation.
